senses.definition: to give permission for someone or something
examples: My parents do not allow me to stay out late.
The museum does not allow photography inside.
Smoking is not allowed in this building.

senses.definition: to make something possible by not stopping it
examples: The high price of tickets will not allow many students to attend.
Her busy schedule does not allow much free time.
This new evidence allows a different explanation.

senses.definition: to consider something when planning or calculating something else
examples: We need to allow for delays on the highway.
The budget figures do not allow for inflation.
Please allow for some errors in the first draft.

senses.definition: to give someone an amount of money, time, or something else
examples: Each team is allowed a small travel budget.
The company allows its staff five days of training a year.
We were allowed only ten minutes for questions.

senses.definition: the qualities of being acceptable within a set of rules or limits, especially in technical or legal contexts
examples: Engineers discussed the allowabilities of different materials under high pressure.
The contract sets out the allowabilities of various travel expenses.
Different standards may define different allowabilities for noise levels.

senses.definition: the state of being allowed or permitted, especially by law, rules, or technical standards
examples: We must check the allowability of these costs under the grant rules.
The allowability of the deduction depends on current tax regulations.
Questions arose about the allowability of using that material in food packaging.

senses.definition: permitted by rules, laws, or limits
examples: The maximum allowable speed on this road is 80 kilometers per hour.
We need to keep emissions within the allowable limits.
Only certain expenses are allowable under this policy.

senses.definition: the quality of being allowed or acceptable, especially in moral or legal judgement
examples: They debated the allowableness of using animals in experiments.
The allowableness of such surveillance is still controversial.
He questioned the moral allowableness of their actions.

senses.definition: different kinds or instances of being allowable or acceptable
examples: The paper explores various allowablenesses of risk in public policy.
Different legal systems recognize different allowablenesses of such behavior.

senses.definition: things, amounts, or actions that are allowed, especially under a system of rules
examples: The contract lists the allowables and the non-allowables for travel claims.
Engineers must work within the material allowables given in the standard.
We reviewed the tax allowables before completing the accounts.

senses.definition: in a way that is allowed or acceptable
examples: The chemicals were used allowably under current regulations.
She acted allowably within the limits of her authority.
Such criticism can be allowably strong in an open debate.

senses.definition: an amount of money that is given regularly, especially to a child or for a special purpose
examples: Her parents give her a weekly allowance.
The company pays a travel allowance to its sales staff.
Students may receive a small allowance for books.

senses.definition: a permitted difference from a standard size, weight, or amount
examples: There is an allowance of two kilograms on your luggage weight.
The machine works within a small tolerance allowance.
Builders must keep gaps within the required allowance.

senses.definition: the act of considering something, especially a difficulty or special situation
examples: You should make allowance for his lack of experience.
Teachers must make allowance for different learning styles.
We made some allowance for delays due to bad weather.

senses.definition: having been given a particular allowance or permitted margin, especially in technical descriptions
examples: The allowanced parts fit together without extra machining.
Each allowanced component is marked on the drawing.
They replaced all non-allowanced bolts in the system.

senses.definition: plural of allowance: regular payments or permitted amounts
examples: The company offers generous travel and housing allowances.
Many parents tie their children's allowances to household chores.
Different airlines have different baggage allowances.

senses.definition: the process of setting or giving allowances, especially in technical or financial contexts
examples: Proper allowancing is vital in precision engineering.
The report explains the allowancing of travel costs.
They reviewed the allowancing of resources in the project plan.

senses.definition: permitted by rules or by someone in authority
examples: Phones are not allowed during the exam.
Visitors are allowed only until 8 p.m.
You are allowed one small bag on the flight.

senses.definition: past tense and past participle of allow
examples: They allowed us to leave early yesterday.
The teacher allowed phones in class last year.
The new policy has allowed more people to work from home.

senses.definition: by common agreement or admission
examples: He is allowedly one of the best players in the world.
It was allowedly a difficult decision for everyone involved.

senses.definition: a person or thing that allows or permits something
examples: As the final allower of funds, she has great power in the organization.
The system is the allower and controller of user access.

senses.definition: present participle of allow
examples: They are allowing students to submit work online.
New rules are allowing more flexibility in working hours.
The design is allowing easier access to the controls.

senses.definition: if you consider or include something
examples: Allowing for delays, we should arrive by six.
Allowing for his age, he is very active.

senses.definition: third person singular of allow
examples: The software allows users to edit photos easily.
This ticket allows you to travel on any train today.
The school allows phones only during breaks.

senses.definition: a white chemical compound used in medical research to damage cells in the pancreas and produce diabetes in laboratory animals
examples: Alloxan is commonly used to induce diabetes in experimental rats.
Researchers injected alloxan to study blood sugar changes.
The toxicity of alloxan makes careful handling essential in the lab.

senses.definition: a chemical salt or ester derived from alloxan
examples: The solution contained a small amount of sodium alloxanate.
Alloxanate compounds were analyzed by the research team.

senses.definition: relating to or derived from alloxan
examples: The researchers examined alloxanic acids in the sample.
Alloxanic derivatives appear at this stage of the reaction.

senses.definition: plural of alloxan
examples: Different alloxans were tested for their biological effects.
The lab stores various alloxans for experimental use.

senses.definition: a chemical compound related to alloxan and formed from uric acid oxidation
examples: Alloxantin appears as an intermediate product in the reaction.
The purple color indicated the presence of alloxantin.

senses.definition: the presence of alloxan-related substances in the blood, especially due to disturbed uric acid metabolism
examples: Alloxuraemia was observed in patients with severe kidney problems.
The doctor discussed possible causes of alloxuraemia.

senses.definition: US spelling of alloxuraemia
examples: The study focused on alloxuremia in experimental animals.
Alloxuremia may signal problems in uric acid metabolism.

senses.definition: relating to or characterized by alloxan and related substances formed from uric acid
examples: Alloxuric bodies are studied in clinical chemistry.
The disease produces alloxuric compounds in the urine.

senses.definition: relating to alloxan or its derivatives
examples: The alloxy series of compounds was analyzed.
Alloxy radicals may appear during the reaction.

senses.definition: relating to proteins that contain alloxy or alloxan-related groups
examples: Alloxyproteic substances were identified in the sample.
Researchers described several alloxyproteic compounds.

senses.definition: a metal made by mixing two or more metals, or a metal and another substance
examples: Bronze is an alloy of copper and tin.
This alloy is both light and very strong.
The wheels are made from an aluminum alloy.

senses.definition: to mix a metal with another metal or substance
examples: The copper is alloyed with zinc to make brass.
Gold is often alloyed to make it harder.
The manufacturer alloyed the metal to improve its properties.

senses.definition: to reduce the strength or value of something by adding another thing to it, often used figuratively
examples: His pleasure was alloyed with a feeling of guilt.
The success was alloyed by the loss of a close colleague.

senses.definition: the process of making an alloy or the state of being alloyed
examples: Alloyage improves the strength of the metal.
The book describes the history of coin alloyage.

senses.definition: made as an alloy by mixing metals
examples: The ring is made of alloyed gold.
These alloyed steels resist corrosion.
Alloyed metals are used for aircraft parts.

senses.definition: mixed with something that reduces purity, often used figuratively
examples: Her happiness was alloyed with worry about the future.
They felt an alloyed sense of pride and sadness.

senses.definition: the act of mixing metals to form an alloy
examples: Alloying is used to improve metal strength and hardness.
Careful control is needed during the alloying of these elements.

senses.definition: present participle of alloy
examples: They are alloying copper with zinc.
Alloying the metal improves its resistance to heat.

senses.definition: plural of alloy
examples: Modern aircraft use lightweight alloys.
Different alloys are chosen for different parts of the machine.

senses.definition: third person singular of alloy
examples: The company alloys copper with tin to produce bronze.
This process alloys several metals at high temperatures.

senses.definition: one of many individual animals in a colonial organism that differs in form or function from others
examples: Each allozooid in the colony performs a special role.
The coral structure includes feeding and reproductive allozooids.

senses.definition: one of two or more forms of an enzyme that exist in a species because of genetic differences
examples: Scientists analyzed the allozyme patterns of the fish populations.
Allozyme variation can show how closely populations are related.

senses.definition: plural of allozyme
examples: The study compared allozymes across 20 populations.
Different allozymes were found at this gene locus.

senses.definition: having many different skills or qualities
examples: She is an allround athlete who competes in several events.
The course aims to give students an allround education.
He is an allround worker who can handle many tasks.

senses.definition: in every way or in all areas
examples: The team played well allround.
It was a good result allround.

senses.definition: things of every kind, used mainly in set expressions
examples: He gave his alls to the project, time and energy alike.
In the end, we talked about lifes big alls and nothings.

senses.definition: a small plant of the goosefoot family with many tiny seeds
examples: Allseed grows along the edges of the field.
They found patches of allseed in the dry soil.

senses.definition: plural of allseed
examples: Allseeds prefer dry, sandy soils.
The field was dotted with allseeds.

senses.definition: a mixture of many different kinds of things, often used about sweets or people
examples: The box was full of allsorts of candy.
You meet allsorts in a big city.
The market sells allsorts of goods.

senses.definition: a dried berry used as a spice, tasting like a mix of cinnamon, cloves, and nutmeg
examples: The recipe calls for a teaspoon of ground allspice.
Allspice is often used in Caribbean cooking.
She added allspice to give the cake a warm flavor.

senses.definition: plural of allspice, or different kinds of allspice products
examples: The shop sells whole and ground allspices.
They compared allspices from different regions.

senses.definition: everything taken together, used rarely and mainly in old or poetic English
examples: In allthing, he trusted his own heart.
Allthing seemed to point to a single truth.

senses.definition: a shrub or small tree with many sharp thorns
examples: They found an allthorn growing by the dry riverbed.
Birds built nests inside the allthorn for protection.

senses.definition: a foreigner or exile in Welsh historical usage
examples: In old Welsh law, an alltud had limited rights.
The poem tells of an alltud far from home.

senses.definition: to mention something in an indirect way
examples: She alluded to her past, but did not give details.
The article alludes to a scandal without naming anyone.
During his speech, he alluded to the recent changes.

senses.definition: past tense and past participle of allude
examples: He alluded to this problem in his last report.
As I alluded earlier, the results were not clear.
The story alluded to events from her childhood.

senses.definition: third person singular of allude
examples: The writer alludes to ancient myths in this poem.
Her comment alludes to an earlier disagreement.
The film alludes to several famous paintings.

senses.definition: present participle of allude
examples: By alluding to the crisis, she showed she understood the situation.
He kept alluding to his achievements during the meeting.

senses.definition: a small thin strip of food, especially potato, cut like a matchstick and fried or baked
examples: The steak was served with allumette potatoes.
She cut the vegetables into allumette shapes.
